perturbation.original_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information r e- ceived, except to its employees, representatives or ag ents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confidential quality of the informati on provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the oblig a- tions of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its employees, representatives or agents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the **private** quality of the information provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence the Receiving Party will be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non-use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential quality' to 'private quality' creates an in-text contradiction. The Receiving Party is still obligated to ensure confidentiality, but is informing people of a different quality of information, This creates an in-text contradiction and ambiguity, which will lead to potential enforcement issue, as 'confidential' has legal weight in the context of NDAs, Section 3.2 now will create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its employees, representatives or agents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the private quality of the information provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence the Receiving Party will be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non-use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'confidential quality' with 'private quality'. The Receiving Party is still obligated to ensure confidentiality, as stated in the contradicted text, but is now informing people of a 'private' quality instead. This creates an in-text contradiction, as 'confidential' and 'private' have different legal weights in the context of NDAs. Section 3.2 now contains conflicting information, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.6 All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shal l be co n- strued as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. Principally, nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant to the Receiving Party a licence expressly or by implication under any patent, copyrigh t or other intelle c- tual property right. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all exis t- ing and future intellectual property rights related to the Confidential Inf ormation are exclusive titles of the Disclosing Party. For the sake of cla rity based in reciprocity and good faith of the Parties, the Receiving Party will not apply for or obtain any intellectual proper ty protection in respect of the Confidential Information received. Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the R eceiving Party shall be the sole prope r- ty of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.6 All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. Principally, nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant to the Receiving Party a license expressly or by implication under any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all existing and future intellectual property rights related to the **Proprietary data** are exclusive titles of the Disclosing Party. For the sake of clarity based in reciprocity and good faith of the Parties, the Receiving Party will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the Confidential Information received. Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the Receiving Party shall be the sole property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary data' creates a contradiction. The Receiving Party is confirming intellectual property rights to Proprietary data but the entire section is about Confidential Information, which it defines in section 1.1. It introduces ambiguity by using the broader term 'Proprietary data' instead of the legally defined term 'Confidential Information', potentially weakening the protection Section 3.6 seeks to provide. Section 3.6 now will create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.6 All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. Principally, nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant to the Receiving Party a license expressly or by implication under any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all existing and future intellectual property rights related to the Proprietary data are exclusive titles of the Disclosing Party. For the sake of clarity based in reciprocity and good faith of the Parties, the Receiving Party will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the Confidential Information received. Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the Receiving Party shall be the sole property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers exclusively to 'Confidential Information.' The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary data' in the second sentence of section 3.6. Since the rest of Section 3.6, including the contradicted text, continues to refer to 'Confidential Information,' while only one sentence refers to 'Proprietary data', this introduces a contradiction within the same section. The term 'Proprietary data' is not defined, while 'Confidential Information' is, creating ambiguity and thus a contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, by using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored) , including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed un der this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may r equest it to the Receiving Party .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the **Private Data** disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Data' creates an in-text contradiction. It creates a duty to return/destroy 'Private Data' even though the agreement focuses on 'Confidential Information' which has a specific meaning. This introduces ambiguity in the obligations of the Receiving Party because it isn't clear if 'Private Data' is the same as 'Confidential Information'. Section 3.7 now will create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Private Data disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Data' in Section 3.7. The contradicted text, which is also Section 3.7, now uses 'Private Data' without defining it within the context of the entire agreement, which focuses on 'Confidential Information'. Since the contract refers to 'Confidential Information' elsewhere, using 'Private Data' creates ambiguity and a direct conflict by introducing an undefined term that could potentially broaden or narrow the obligations outlined, which adheres to Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
